channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
item.description: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
item.content:encoded: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Trump earned more than $1 billion from crypto — but his investors didn't fare so well
item.description: President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures. But there are thousands of investors who bought into some of those cryptocurrencies, and they stand to lose big.
item.content:encoded: President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures. But there are thousands of investors who bought into some of those cryptocurrencies, and they stand to lose big.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: U.S. job market slows in June
item.description: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
item.content:encoded: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Meta considered buying Kalshi before developing its own prediction market app
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.
item.content:encoded:
Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon/AFP and Aaron Schwartz/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: How to eat out without spending a fortune
item.description: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out on a budget. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
item.content:encoded: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out on a budget. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Computer scientist on why he believes mass layoffs due to AI is a 'convenient excuse'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Princeton computer scientist Sayash Kapoor about his assertions that AI won't lead to mass layoffs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Princeton computer scientist Sayash Kapoor about his assertions that AI won't lead to mass layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
item.description: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.
item.content:encoded:
In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
item.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
item.content:encoded:
A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.

(Image credit: Valar Atomics)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: This mindset shift can help you get better at using up your leftovers
item.description: Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.
item.content:encoded:
Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.

(Image credit: Pulse/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Emily Siner

item.title: World Cup fans are missing games after their resale tickets fall through
item.description: The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.
item.content:encoded:
The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.

(Image credit: Shafkat Anowar)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim
